purpose: Reference index for converting ARPG camera, animation, enemy AI, and VFX observations into IsekaiBrawl Story PvE design tasks.

scopeGuard.summary: This dataset is a seed catalog, not a limit. Main-PC follow-up may add more ARPG games, footage studies, frame tags, community databases, GitHub datasets, official guides, and extracted schema observations.

baselineConstraints: latest user correction treats the game as landscape / horizontal ARPG-facing
horizontal combat camera may use soft lock-on, target-relative framing, and short impact shots
summon-first combat remains a differentiator but should not block stronger ARPG mechanics
dodge counter, assist entry, melee combo beats, boss pattern phases, and stronger VFX layers are valid research candidates
old lane-first and hand-of-cards prototype assumptions should not silently return
camera motion patterns, timing windows, state graphs, observed tuning ranges, and VFX layer recipes are valid reference data
do not import proprietary meshes, textures, animation clips, audio, source code, or full raw tables as project assets

sourceRecords.notes: Mechanic relationship source: 3-Ping, QTE, EX Dodge, Matrix, shields, roles, energy, signature move.
Matrix confirms perfect-dodge time-slow counter window.
Archived public repo. Use schemas, action names, timing patterns, and observed ranges as reference; do not import raw tables as assets.
Useful for skill categories and cancel/counter grammar.
Time Fracture slows time and round timers; for IsekaiBrawl use only presentation, not timer manipulation.
Public extracted data. Use schemas, behavior separation, and observed ranges as reference; do not import raw tables as assets.
Best public source found for enemy daze gauge, stun duration, and stun damage multiplier examples.
Dodge, Perfect Dodge, Dodge Counter, dash state, projectile deflect examples.
Chain Attack and Ultimate invulnerability/trigger grammar.
Public extracted data. Use camera cue structure, effect layer order, and observed timing as reference; do not import raw tables as assets.
Unreleased preview-level data only; no raw combat data found.
Useful horizontal ARPG comparison from Kuro Games. Use combat grammar and timing concepts as reference.
Thin source, but useful as a low-confidence team-action positioning reference.

zzzWitchTimeDodgeStructure.isekaiTranslation: Use named start/end effect layers and explicit cleanup. The observed 4s duration is valid reference data, but IsekaiBrawl should retune it for its own combat tempo.

zzzAnimationCameraStructure.isekaiTranslation: Trigger short camera cues from combat or animation events as additive modifiers over authored BattleCamera values. 18-41 frames is a useful observed range for counter, assist, and heavy-hit emphasis before retuning.

pgrCameraParamStructure.isekaiTranslation: Use bounded named camera contexts or cue presets; avoid runtime scene-name camera branching.

pgrNpcAnimationActions.isekaiTranslation: Keep enemy and summon animation actions as a small taxonomy with explicit trigger/state semantics.

pgrAffixPatterns.behavior: movement speed increases when farther from target
pgrAffixPatterns.recommendedUse: anti-camping support enemy or boss reinforcement

pgrAffixPatterns.behavior: periodic shield based on max HP

pgrAffixPatterns.recommendedUse: creates target-priority reason for Arrow or Break

pgrAffixPatterns.behavior: teleport depending on distance to target

hi3MonsterConfigStructure.isekaiTranslation: Separate enemy identity, stats, behavior package, and presentation cue data.

hi3AttackPunishCurve.isekaiTranslation: The threshold shape is valid tuning reference. Use it only if the updated ARPG scope needs difficulty clamps or RPG scaling.

pgrMatrixMechanic.mechanic: perfect dodge creates Matrix time-slow and a Matrix-Ping opportunity
pgrMatrixMechanic.isekaiTranslation: Perfect dodge can grant micro time emphasis, energy pulse, or summon-read clarity without adding new input.

observedClaims: no confirmed release date or platforms in the article
silverPalacePreview.isekaiTranslation: Market/aesthetic reference only until real combat data becomes available.

observedClaims: Intro skills trigger on combat entry or character switch-in
Outro skills trigger on combat exit or character switch-out
Echoes can briefly summon or transform characters into monsters
Traversal/combat includes aerial movement, dodges, and parries
Tune Break is described as a later feature where repeated attacks can open a special enemy state for damage and attack cancel
wutheringWavesGameplayGrammar.isekaiTranslation: Treat summon entry like an Intro skill, summon exit/cooldown handoff like an Outro skill, and some summons like short Echo-style manifestations.

observedClaims: character skillset knowledge, power-ups, combos, and squad customization are emphasized
Modifiers are the core combat actors
aetherGazerTeamActionNote.isekaiTranslation: Low-confidence support for team-action framing: keep summon and helper identity visible in combat rather than hiding allies as pure stats.

recommendedIsekaiPatterns.summary: Add short-lived camera cues driven by combat or animation events, including horizontal ARPG soft-lock and impact framing.
implementationNotes: Base camera should remain authored on scene BattleCamera until root docs are synced.
Cue must preserve target and threat readability.
Landscape presets can include ExplorationFollow, CombatSoftLock, BossPressure, DodgeCounter, SummonEntry, and UltimateImpact.
Cue owns cleanup for zoom, shake, stretch, and screen effects.
Avoid only persistent uncontrolled orbit that hides threats or confuses input direction.

recommendedIsekaiPatterns.summary: Reusable horizontal ARPG camera movement patterns extracted from reference games.

patterns.use: heavy attacks, summon impact, counter hit
perfect dodge counter and parry reward
boss phase shift or pattern deck change

recommendedIsekaiPatterns.summary: Translate enemy daze/stun into relief and punish windows suitable for horizontal ARPG combat.
implementationNotes: Small enemy: brief interrupt.
Elite/support pressure: 2-4 second pocket shift.
Early boss: pressure-only brief interrupt, not HP kill.
Final boss: real HP kill with summons allowed to contribute.
If input scope expands, stun can enable launch, back attack, counter chain, or summon assist follow-up.

recommendedIsekaiPatterns.summary: Implementation-facing modules for horizontal ARPG boss and elite enemy pattern decks.
Use a first-pass horizontal ARPG subset of enemy behavior modules to create summon, dodge, and counter reasons.
implementationNotes: Start with ShieldCycle, AuraBuffer, LinePressure, DeathBomb, SummonPackage.
Add ARPG candidates when readability supports them: ParryBait, ArmorBreak, Launcher, ClosePunish, RetreatShot, PhaseSwap.
Treat invisibility, clone split, rigid body, full cage, and laser spam as watchlist modules, not permanently banned modules.
Each module should map to a likely summon answer.

recommendedIsekaiPatterns.summary: Reward perfect dodge with clarity and tempo, not extra controls.
implementationNotes: Use micro time emphasis and small energy pulse.
Optionally flicker a top-band oracle hint.
Do not slow the run timer unless the broader ARPG direction explicitly adopts timer manipulation.
If input scope expands, Perfect Dodge can enable a counter hit, assist entry, or short target lock-on snap.
Do not add Matrix/orb/hand UI by default.

recommendedIsekaiPatterns.summary: All screen effects must have named start/end cleanup.
implementationNotes: Use small layers: vignette, radial blur, color adjustment, screen flash.
Avoid stacking persistent full-screen effects in mobile combat.
Summon/structure success should create a visible but brief relief cue.

recommendedIsekaiPatterns.summary: Normalize enemy/summon animation actions around readable state and trigger semantics, then expand for ARPG combat beats.
implementationNotes: Minimum actions: Idle, Advance, Retreat, Windup, Attack, Recover, HitReact, Stagger, Death, SummonCall.
Record cancel windows for player basic fire, dodge, ultimate, and summon call.
Add PerfectCounter, Launch, SlamImpact, GuardBreak, AssistEntry, and BossPhaseShift when the horizontal ARPG scope needs them.

recommendedIsekaiPatterns.summary: Support horizontal ARPG readability with soft lock-on and target-priority camera logic.
implementationNotes: Prefer soft target assist over hard manual target selection unless the UI is deliberately redesigned.
Camera priority can rank boss, active attacker, projectile source, and current player target.
Short target snaps are allowed for dodge counter, guard break, summon impact, and ultimate.
Input direction must remain predictable after camera pivots.

recommendedIsekaiPatterns.summary: Author bosses as phase-based pattern decks instead of one monolithic behavior.
implementationNotes: Separate identity, stats, behavior package, telegraph, VFX, and camera cue data.
Pattern deck examples: LinePressure, ClosePunish, RetreatShot, SummonPackage, GuardBreak, PhaseShift.
Use cooldowns and recovery windows so the fight does not collapse into nonstop dodge churn.
Each phase should create a clear summon or counter timing question.

recommendedIsekaiPatterns.summary: Create stronger ARPG hit feel through coordinated animation, hit-stop, VFX, and camera.
implementationNotes: Use short hit-stop for perfect dodge counter, guard break, ultimate, and heavy summon impact.
Use named VFX layers with explicit cleanup.
Avoid persistent screen clutter during normal projectile pressure.
Pair impact with enemy pose change, not only particles.

recommendedIsekaiPatterns.summary: Named VFX layer recipes for ARPG-readable combat events.

recipes.timing: start on dodge success; 0.15-0.35s hit-stop; optional 0.6-1.2s clarity tail
starts immediately after dodge window; camera returns before next threat
crack stacks build over hits; break impact gets stronger cue
1-2s presentation can be acceptable if control state is clear
strongest cue; avoid long clutter after damage

doNotAdopt.item: PGR orb ping and Matrix-Ping UI
doNotAdopt.reason: It is too specific to PGR. Translate the reward into dodge counter, energy, assist, or summon-entry timing instead.

doNotAdopt.item: Honkai character swap/QTE as direct control model
doNotAdopt.reason: IsekaiBrawl v1 uses summons, not active party swapping.
Use the daze/relief grammar first. Chain-like assists can be considered later if the horizontal ARPG input model expands.
Short target-relative ARPG camera cues are allowed; the risk is a persistent orbit that hides threats or breaks input readability.

nextTasks: Draft first-pass horizontal ARPG StoryPveEnemyPatternModule list.
Draft PerfectDodgeReward design note with micro time emphasis and energy pulse.
Capture 5-10 short gameplay clips per target game and tag camera events by frame.
Prototype one horizontal ARPG camera preset: CombatSoftLockFollow + CounterSnap + UltimateImpactHold.
